app.name: FlareDeck
app.description: Local development publish manager for Cloudflare Tunnel

dashboard.emptyBody: Create a profile to map a cloudflared tunnel to FlareDeck. Each profile keeps its own ~/.cloudflared/<id>.yml and can run independently.

proxy.noRoutes: No ingress rules yet. Add a route to map a public hostname to a local origin.
proxy.routeDescription: Maps a public hostname to a local origin. The catch-all rule is appended automatically.
proxy.serviceScheme: must start with http:// or https://

profile.newDescription: A profile maps to a named cloudflared tunnel. Make sure you've signed in to Cloudflare first (Settings → Sign in).
profile.createTunnel: Create the tunnel on Cloudflare now
profile.createTunnelHelp: Runs `cloudflared tunnel create` and seeds the config YAML with its UUID. Uncheck if the tunnel already exists.
profile.wslHostHelp: Rewrites localhost to the WSL VM IP in the saved config so cloudflared (Windows-side) can reach the origin.
profile.wslHostHelpDetected: WSL IP detected: {{ip}}. Saved config will use this address.
profile.signInRequired: Sign in to Cloudflare first (Settings → Sign in).

config.title: Configuration
config.selectProfile: Select or create a profile on the dashboard to edit its config.

settings.cloudflaredDescription: Local CLI used to manage tunnels.
settings.accountDescription: Signs in via cloudflared tunnel login.
settings.browserHint: Complete sign in in your browser; we will detect cert.pem.
settings.wslDescription: When a service lives inside WSL Ubuntu, FlareDeck rewrites localhost to the WSL VM IP so Windows-side cloudflared can reach it.
settings.profilesDescription: Each profile maps to a named cloudflared tunnel with its own config YAML.
settings.profilesEmpty: No profiles yet. Create one from the dashboard.
settings.profileDeleteConfirm: Delete profile "{{name}}"? The cloudflared tunnel itself stays on Cloudflare; only FlareDeck's profile entry and its config YAML are removed.
settings.windowDescription: Control what happens when you close the FlareDeck window.
settings.minimizeToTray: Keep running in the background when I close the window
settings.minimizeToTrayHelp: FlareDeck hides to the Windows system tray instead of quitting. Right-click the tray icon to bring the window back or quit. Tunnels keep running.

tray.hiddenBody: Closed window hides FlareDeck to the system tray. Right-click the tray icon to bring it back or quit. Disable this in Settings → Window.

closePrompt.title: Keep FlareDeck running?
closePrompt.body: Choose what happens when you close the window. Tunnels keep running either way until you Quit. You can change this later in Settings → Window.
